entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: The deterioration of the environmental quality of land, affecting soil, forests, biodiversity, and water.

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The upper layer of earth in which plants grow, affected by land degradation.

entities.name: FORESTS
entities.description: Large areas covered chiefly with trees and undergrowth, impacted by land degradation and deforestation.

entities.name: BIODIVERSITY
entities.description: The variety of plant and animal life in the world or in a particular habitat.

entities.name: WATER
entities.description: The liquid resource essential for life, affected by degradation and runoff.

entities.name: SOCIO-ECONOMIC SERVICES
entities.description: Services derived from the ecosystem that support human social and economic well-being.

entities.name: LAND USE
entities.description: The management and modification of natural environment into built environment such as settlements and agricultural land.

entities.name: DEFORESTATION
entities.description: The action of clearing a wide area of trees.

entities.name: RUNOFF
entities.description: The draining away of water from the surface of an area of land.

entities.name: SILTATION
entities.description: The process by which water becomes dirty as a result of fine mineral particles in the water.

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: A condition where the amount of water available is less than the amount required.

entities.name: FLOODING
entities.description: An overflowing of a large amount of water beyond its normal confines.

entities.name: EUTROPHICATION
entities.description: Excessive richness of nutrients in a lake or other body of water, frequently due to runoff.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: A long-term change in the average weather patterns that have come to define Earth's local, regional and global climates.

entities.name: PLANETARY BOUNDARY CONDITIONS
entities.description: Global limits within which humanity can safely operate.

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability to be maintained at a certain rate or level, specifically regarding natural resources.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The effectiveness of converting inputs into outputs in the landscape.

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: The state of being extremely poor, coinciding with degraded landscapes.

entities.name: MALNUTRITION
entities.description: Lack of proper nutrition, caused by lack of resource availability in degraded areas.

entities.name: MIGRATION
entities.description: Movement from one area or country to settle in another, often driven by poverty.

entities.name: UNEMPLOYMENT
entities.description: The state of being without a job, resulting from precarious socio-economic conditions.

entities.name: LANDSCAPE RESOURCE CONSERVATION
entities.description: Science-based approaches to harvest surface runoff and conserve resources.

entities.name: ENGINEERING MEASURES
entities.description: Technical interventions used for landscape resource conservation.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL MEASURES
entities.description: Nature-based interventions used for landscape resource conservation.

entities.name: WATER RETENTION ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of the landscape to hold water in surface and groundwater systems.

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE INTENSIFICATION
entities.description: Increasing agricultural yields without adverse environmental impact.

entities.name: AGRI-FOOD SYSTEM
entities.description: The entire range of activities involved in the production, processing, and distribution of food.

entities.name: ODISHA
entities.description: A state in India where the study is focused.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study is conducted.
